method_atom: A scheme for extending a multiplicative-type scalar functional (a regularized product-invariant) to a large class of non-commuting linear operators, and for quantifying its failure of multiplicativity on products: the anomaly is identified with a central extension of the underlying symmetry group together with an invariant quadratic form on that extension, whose infinitesimal (Lie-algebra) structure is read off from associated leading-order data. The construction rests on a new trace-type linear functional defined on operators of non-integer order, which regularizes the functional and yields a canonical, holomorphically-varying value whose monodromy is constrained to a finite group of roots of unity.
object_atom: Elliptic pseudo-differential and differential operators acting on a closed manifold (especially odd-dimensional ones near positive-definite operators), and the group of their elliptic symbols, whose zeta-regularized determinants are the objects being studied.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the group-cohomological anomaly machinery (central extension + invariant quadratic form) and trace-type regularization measuring multiplicativity failure; the domain-specific part is elliptic operators on manifolds and their symbol group, which are somewhat fused with the method since the trace functional is defined specifically for pseudo-differential operators.
